title: The AI layoff trap: Why replacing workers with AI could hurt companies too
description: A March 2026 paper titled “The AI Layoff Trap” by economists Brett Hemenway Falk of the University of Pennsylvania and Gerry Tsoukalas of Boston University argues that firms may be walking into what they call an “automation arms race.” Their key message is simple: when companies replace too many workers with AI, they also reduce the number of consumers who can afford to buy goods and services. Over time, that shrinking demand can damage the very firms doing the automating.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Responds to AI Chip Claims with Humor
description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the young star of Rajasthan Royals, humorously responded to claims by Pakistani analyst Dr. Nauman Niaz about an 'AI chip' in his bat. Following his explosive performance in the IPL, where he scored 43 runs off 16 balls, Sooryavanshi attributed his hitting prowess to divine talent. His witty reply has gone viral, earning him praise from fans and cricket enthusiasts alike. With over 400 runs this season, he has set records as the first teenager to achieve this milestone in IPL history. Discover more about his remarkable journey and the social media buzz surrounding his response.

title: Your next iPhone upgrade is going to hurt your wallet, and AI is to blame
description: Apple's incoming CEO faces a nightmare decision: absorb a 400% memory cost spike driven by AI demand, or pass it on to you.

title: AI knocks on the door of one in four jobs, BofA says
description: Bank of America says 24% of global jobs face generative AI exposure, highest in rich economies.

title: Healthcare executives push for wider patient use of AI diagnostics while regulatory and bias concerns persist
description: Healthcare executives are pushing for broader patient use of AI diagnostic tools, even as issues around accuracy, bias, liability, and regulation remain unsettled. Medical groups and patient advocates have raised concerns about moving too fast.
